message: PROOF-REVIEW LOOP ESCALATION [unclear]: [scaffold-mismatch] `AdaptedCADConstruction` (Helpers/CADInterface.lean) quantifies over EVERY Nodup `order : List (Fin r)` covering all variables, but the cell structure it demands mixes two DIFFERENT variable orders: `IsRecursivelyLiftedCADCell A order cell` lifts the HEAD of `order` outermost, while `IsAdaptedCAD.cylindrical = IsCylindricallyArranged` (via `cadTruncate k`, prefixes of the NATURAL index order) demands cylindricity over the natural coordinate prefixes. These agree only for `order = [r-1, …, 0]`; for any other order the conjunction is unsatisfiable, so the assumed cited Prop is FALSE, not just over-strong. Concrete refutation: r = 2, A = {X₀ − X₁}, order = [0,1] — sign-invariance for the generated family (which contains −X₁) forces base cells {x₁<0}, {x₁=0}, {x₁>0} and the recursion stacks cells in x₀ over them, so the cells {x₁>0, x₀<x₁} and {x₁<0, x₀<x₁} have coordinate-0 projections ℝ and (−∞,0), which are neither equal nor disjoint; `IsCylindricallyArranged` fails and no admissible family exists. FIX (mechanical, no judgment on the frozen note needed): make cylindricity relative to the SAME order used for lifting — i.e. replace `cadTruncate k` prefixes with prefixes of `order` in `IsCylindricallyArranged` (per BPR's disclosed relabelling normalization), or else drop the ∀-order quantifier and fix `order = (List.finRange r).reverse` (BPR's printed X_k-first elimination). BCR/BPR support only the matched-order form; the note and the cited statement of record are both faithful — only the Lean rendering drifted.
